impacts.statement: Major flooding of lower half of town, adjacent to river. Several businesses along State Route 5 flood.

Water affects several businesses at the intersection of South Lewis Street and Main Street. Some homes along College Street, near the intersection with East Main Street, begin to flood. Several homes on Hunter Street, Brooklyn Drive, and Park Street flood. Gas utility company off State Route 5, adjacent to river, floods.
US Route 119 and 33 are closed, east and west of town. Portions of West Main Street along with the intersection with South Lewis Street flood.
Backwater flooding from Town Run covers most of South Street and affects a few homes and and business. A few homes adjacent to the river on River Street flood.
Backwater flooding on Town Run covers portions of South Street and affects home. Home on Brooklyn Drive adjacent to river floods.
Most of Elm Street floods. Water reaches the hotel foundation on Conrad Court next to the old 1885 bridge.
Low spots on Brooklyn Drive, Elm Street, and River Street flood.
Water begins to cover low spots on Brooklyn Drive, Elm Street, and River Street.

properties.caption: Home, building, and parking lot next to gage flood at 29 to 30 feet
Several buisnesses along East Main Street flood at 29 to 30 feet
Much of Elm Street floods at 25 feet
Water reaches foundation of hotel next to gage at 25 feet
View of old 1885 bridge adjacent to gage
Powell Street floods at 27 to 28 feet
River Street and a few homes adjacent to the river flood at 27 feet
Businesses at intersection of South Lewis Street and Main Street flood at 29 to 30 feet
Intersection of South Lewis Street and Main Street floods at 27 feet
At 26 feet, backwater flooding on Town Run floods portions of South Street and affects some homes

hydronotes.statement: River Forecasts typically include ONLY 2 days of future rain from <u>forecast issuance time</u> (lower right of graph). Occasionally 3 days of future rain included.

value.label: North American Vertical Datum of 1988
value.description: A fixed reference for elevations determined by geodetic leveling. The datum was derived from a general adjustment of the first-order terrestrial leveling nets of the United States, Canada, and Mexico. In the adjustment, only the height of the primary tidal bench mark, referenced to the International Great Lakes Datum of 1985 (IGLD 85) local mean sea level height value, at Father Point, Rimouski, Quebec, Canada was held fixed, thus providing minimum constraint. NAVD 88 and IGLD 85 are identical. However, NAVD 88 bench mark values are given in Helmert orthometric height units while IGLD 85 values are in dynamic heights. See International Great Lakes Datum of 1985, National Geodetic Vertical Datum of 1929, and geopotential difference. NAVD 88 should not be used as Mean Sea Level.
